name: Halberstam v. Sestak
summary: A D.C. Circuit decision articulating common law aiding-and-abetting factors, including knowledge of the wrongful conduct and substantial assistance, which Congress reportedly viewed as the standard statement of common law.
significance: Cited by Respondents as the common law standard for aiding-and-abetting liability, which uses knowledge rather than purpose as the intent element, with multiple factors for substantial assistance.
